incorrect.source_says: The paper discusses PSCs generically (both ESCs and iPSCs); it does not designate iPSC as the cell source for any specific protocol. The lung-organoid generation described is repeatedly attributed to hESCs.
incorrect.evidence_quote: PSCs include both embryonic stem cells (ESCs) and induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) [ 34 , 35 , 36 , 37 ]

incorrect.source_says: The authors' own lung-organoid work analyzing DNA damage is explicitly done in minilungs derived from hESCs, contradicting an iPSC designation.
incorrect.evidence_quote: We made use of the advantages of bleomycin to analyze expression of DNA damage in minilungs derived from hESCs.

incorrect.source_says: Matrigel appears only as a parenthetical example of a class of biogels in a Figure 2 schematic caption, not as a matrix used in any concrete protocol with composition or percentage.
incorrect.evidence_quote: Tridimensional structures composed of cells of the airways ( B ) or the alveoli ( C ), derived from PSCs and embedded into biogels (e.g., Matrigel Tm , etc.)

recall_misses.source_says: Dexamethasone is named as a specific agent added to the maturation cocktail to generate the authors' paddle-racquet lung organoids (PRLOs); it is culture-relevant and was omitted from signaling_factors / small_molecules.
recall_misses.evidence_quote: Generation of such PRLOs was achieved via addition of dexamethasone to the maturation cocktail at certain times of differentiation.

recall_misses.source_says: Human fibronectin is named as a substrate for mounting 2D minilungs, a culture-matrix item not captured in the matrix field.
recall_misses.evidence_quote: Bidimensional arrays (2D minilungs) of both airways and lung epithelial cells derived from PSCs mounted on different substrates, such as human fibronectin.

recall_misses.source_says: hESCs are repeatedly given as the stem-cell source for lung organoids, and a specific hESC line (RUES2) is named for one cited protocol; neither was captured (line_name left null, cell_type set to iPSC).
recall_misses.evidence_quote: Han et al. [ 139 ], however, developed human minilungs and colonic organoids from hESCs (RUES2) based on previously reported stepwise strategies.

recall_misses.source_says: A sequential/staged differentiation timeline (definitive endoderm, then progenitors, then maturation) is described but the timeline field is empty.
recall_misses.evidence_quote: these protocols depend on generation of definitive endoderm and generation of progenitors ... lung progenitors that are subsequently matured by addition of fibroblast growth factors

notes: SOURCE is a REVIEW article (Int J Mol Sci 2023, MDPI), not a primary protocol paper. It contains NO methods/protocol section with concrete reagents, NO tables (tables=[]), and NO supplementary text (empty); the 2 figures are schematic illustrations whose graphics were not read (multimodal=false). All extracted signaling factors (Activin A, TGFb, WNT, NOTCH, BMP4, Wnt3a, CHI99021, Noggin, FGF2, FGF7, FGF10, BMP4) are verbatim-faithful to the source's generic narrative of pathways 'commonly used' across the field, with no concentrations (the paper gives none) -- these quote-checks are CORRECT as transcriptions. CHI99021 reproduces the paper's own (mis)spelling of CHIR99021. The extraction's organoid_type='lung' is correct (the bundle's own organoid_type metadata field erroneously says 'intestinal'). Main errors: (1) cell_type asserted as iPSC when the paper's source is generically PSCs and its own experiments use hESCs; (2) Matrigel promoted to a protocol matrix when it is only a parenthetical figure-caption example. Recall misses are minor given the review nature: dexamethasone, human fibronectin substrate, the RUES2 hESC line, and the staged endoderm->progenitor->maturation timeline. Because the article carries no quantitative protocol (no doses/timelines/media to validate), this document is poorly suited as a silver-gold protocol-extraction target.
